summary: Rust ↔ Go cross-language interop at Level 2 (frame-level). Go implementation is a wire-format library (no QUIC transport), so Level 1 (live QUIC) is not possible. 7 Rust tests spawn the Go fixture generator, decode Go-produced CBOR/frame/handshake/AgentRecord fixtures in Rust, and verify byte-for-byte equality after re-encoding. All 39 fixtures pass. Additionally, 48 Go test vector tests, 17 golden trace cross-verifications, and ML-DSA-65 cross-signature verification (19/19 + 15/15 + 100/100) all pass. Stale Go fixtures were regenerated to include A-3 record_version and A-4 session_id binding.

details.notes: Go implementation has no QUIC transport layer (explicitly transport-agnostic). Level 1 (live QUIC interop) not possible. Level 2 (frame-level) is the appropriate interop level.
All Go tests pass: 48 test vectors, 17 golden traces, handshake, identity, frame, closemanager, pipeline, replaycache, mldsa, versionneg, racestress, interop

Regenerated stale Go interop fixtures. Previous fixtures were missing A-3 record_version (key 10) in AgentRecord and A-4 server_agent_id binding in session ID. After regeneration, 39/39 fixtures pass in Rust.
16 CBOR fixtures: Go encodes, Rust decodes and re-encodes, bytes match. Covers uint, negative, bool, null, bstr, tstr, empty array/map, int map, string map.
6 frame fixtures: DATA (empty, with payload, with flags), HANDSHAKE, RPC_REQUEST, PING. Go encodes, Rust decodes and re-encodes, bytes match.
3 handshake messages: ClientHello (without sig), ServerHello (without sig), ClientFinished (without sig). Go encodes, Rust decodes, verifies all fields, re-encodes, bytes match.
2 AgentRecord fixtures: without_sig (manual field extraction, key 8 absent) and with_sig (full from_cbor, 3309-byte signature). Both round-trip byte-for-byte.
4 transcript hash stages: init, after ClientHello, after ServerHello, after ClientFinished. Rust independently computes from Go-produced CBOR, values match.
Session ID derived from h_after_clienthello + client_nonce + server_nonce + server_agent_id. Rust independently computes, value matches Go-produced fixture.
6 RPC fixtures: request_basic, request_with_params, response_success, response_error, close_message, error_message_fatal. All round-trip byte-for-byte.
ML-DSA-65 cross-signature verification: 19/19 Rust vectors verify in Go, 15/15 Go vectors verify in Rust, 100/100 diff traces cross-verify (A-10, previously completed).

metrics.go_tests_passed: all (48 test vectors + 17 golden traces + others)
